name: Vaelori
description:
The Vaelori grew not from soil or sea but from the silicon lattices of Aetharis, a tidally-locked world of perpetual twilight. Over billions of years, piezoelectric crystals in the planet's crust developed resonance patterns of staggering complexity — patterns that, given enough geological time, became thought. The Vaelori do not have brains in any biological sense. They are thinking minerals: slow-grown, ancient, and deeply strange. A single Vaelori elder may have been continuously conscious for forty thousand years, its crystalline lattice accumulating memories the way a glacier accumulates stone.

They are expanding into the galaxy because something is wrong with the song. For as long as the Vaelori have existed, they have perceived a psychic resonance woven through the fabric of spacetime — what they call the Lattice Harmonic, a background hum of consciousness that permeates the universe. Three centuries ago, the Harmonic began to shift. New dissonances appeared. Notes of wrongness, coming from beyond the galactic rim. The Vaelori cannot say what is approaching, only that it is vast, it is hungry, and it is getting closer. They have come to the stars not from ambition but from dread — ancient beings stirred to action by a frequency that tastes of extinction.

To other species, the Vaelori are unsettling in the way that a cathedral is unsettling — beautiful, immense, and indifferent to the small concerns of mortal life. They communicate through modulated psionic pulses that lesser minds experience as whispers, visions, or migraines. They do not lie, but they are deeply selective about what they share, and what they withhold could fill libraries. Their culture values contemplation above all else; a Vaelori who acts hastily is regarded the way humans regard someone who screams in a church. They create by thought alone — architecture, art, even their spacecraft are psionic constructs, crystalline forms willed into existence through focused meditation.

Yet the Vaelori are not the serene monolith they present to outsiders. The Fracture — a philosophical schism older than most species' civilisations — divides them into two camps. The Resonants believe the galaxy's species should be warned about the approaching dissonance and prepared for what comes. The Attenuants believe lesser minds are simply too fragile to comprehend the truth, and that sharing it would cause only panic. Some Attenuants go further, arguing that certain species are themselves sources of psychic noise — and that silence would serve the Harmonic better than diplomacy. The Vaelori speak with one voice to the galaxy. Behind that voice, they argue endlessly about whether the galaxy deserves to hear what they know.
